zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Joan Markham於1436年出生在英國英格蘭艾可福可亞特時，她的父親Sir Robert Markham I30歲，母親Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon28歲。她和William De Bures II至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1524年7月10日在英國英格蘭素福爾可布勒斯 聖 瑪麗享年88歲。

nameMeanings.name: Markham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Markham Clinton or East Markham (Nottinghamshire). The placename derives from Old English mearc ‘mark, boundary’ + hām ‘village, homestead’ or hamm ‘water meadow’.

Irish: English surname used as an equivalent of Gaelic Ó Marcacháin ‘descendant of Marcachán’, a diminutive of Marcach ‘horseman, rider’ (see Markey ). This was originally a Galway surname but is now found mainly in Clare, sometimes translated into English as Ryder .

variants.name: Markey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Donovan, Fergal.
Irish (Monaghan): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Marcaigh ‘descendant of Marcach’, a name meaning ‘horseman, knight’, from marc ‘horse’. Compare Markham 2.
English: apparently from a placename meaning ‘boundary river’ in Old or Middle English, but no such place has been identified.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Joan Markham was born in 1436, in Foxearth, Essex, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Robert Markham I, was 30 and her mother, Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon, was 28. She had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with William De Bures II. She died on 10 July 1524, in Bures Saint Mary, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 88.

ko.Bures Saint Mary, Babergh, Suffolk, Engl: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버스 세인트 메리
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Markham(이)가 1436년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 폭세아르스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Markham I(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth de Bourdon(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 William De Bures II(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1524년 7월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버스 세인트 메리에서 88세의 나이로 사망했다.
